question: Did Murthy’s loneliness advisory come before or after Nobel’s AP definition of loneliness?
answer:
Murthy’s loneliness advisory came **before** Nobel’s AP definition of loneliness. Murthy’s advisory is described as having been issued “**last spring**,” when the U.S. surgeon general declared loneliness and isolation an “epidemic” [Data: Sources (5084, 5085, +more)].

Nobel’s definition (“Loneliness is subjective … it’s the gap between the social connections you want to have and the ones you do have.”) appears in an AP story dated **Feb. 10, 2024** [Data: Sources (5094, 5083, +more)].

question: Did the Air Force’s unsafe PCB finding come before its Minuteman III cancer review?
answer:
Yes. The Air Force’s finding of unsafe PCB levels in some Minuteman III launch control capsule locations was disclosed while the broader “missile community cancer review” was still underway and before the Air Force released its first cancer-review numbers. Specifically, the Air Force said that out of more than 2,000 environmental samples at the Montana and Wyoming missile bases, four locations inside underground launch control capsules had unsafe PCB levels, and it was still awaiting results from North Dakota [Data: Sources (8280, 8281, +more)]. In that same update, the Air Force also said it was not yet making its initial cancer-number findings public for “a month or so,” even as it announced it was expanding the medical-record review [Data: Sources (8278, 8282, +more)].

The Air Force later released initial cancer-review data in March 2024 (e.g., reporting 23 identified non-Hodgkin lymphoma cases so far, while noting the data was incomplete) [Data: Sources (3371, 3373, +more)]. This indicates the unsafe PCB finding came first, and the cancer-review results came afterward.

question: Which return-to-work date came first: England’s NHS junior doctors or South Korea’s striking interns?
answer:
South Korea’s striking interns (and other trainee doctors) had the earlier return-to-work date: the South Korean government ordered them to return **by Feb. 29** (with warnings of license suspensions and possible prosecution if they missed it) [Data: Sources (5968, 2517, 6416, 3971, 3931, +more)].

England’s NHS junior doctors’ walkout referenced here was scheduled to end later: they were due to stay off the job until **7 a.m. on Jan. 9** (i.e., returning then, absent any emergency “derogation” recall) [Data: Sources (6554)].

question: What happened first: Buckingham Palace’s cancer statement or Kate’s Windsor video message?
answer:
Kate’s Windsor video message happened first. Kate disclosed her cancer diagnosis in a video message that was recorded in Windsor and broadcast on Friday, March 22, 2024 [Data: Sources (3987, 3988, 4112, 4050, 1311)].

Buckingham Palace’s cancer statement (in which King Charles said he was “so proud of Catherine for her courage in speaking as she did”) was issued in response to Kate’s announcement, meaning it came after her video message [Data: Sources (3990, 3991, 4115, 4114, 4147, +more)].

question: Which happened first: the 11th Circuit’s 59-page opinion or the 3rd Circuit’s mask-refusal ruling?
answer:
The **11th Circuit’s 59-page opinion happened first**.

The 3rd Circuit’s mask-refusal ruling is described as having been issued “Monday,” and the associated AP report was first created **Feb. 6, 2024** [Data: Sources (4756, 4757, 4758, 4759, 4760, +more)]. By contrast, the Florida editorial roundup referencing the 11th Circuit’s 59-page opinion says the 11th Circuit “contributed to the record **this week**,” and that editorial is dated **Jan. 15, 2024** [Data: Sources (7166, 7167)].

So, based on the dates in the provided materials, the 11th Circuit opinion predates the 3rd Circuit mask-refusal ruling [Data: Sources (7166, 7167, 4756, 4757, 4758, +more)].

question: How did the proposed 2,000-seat increase compare with South Korea’s current 3,058 medical school admissions cap?
answer:
South Korea’s proposed increase of **2,000** medical school admission seats would be added on top of the **current annual cap of 3,058** seats. That would raise the cap to **5,058** seats per year if implemented in full. [Data: Sources (3975, 3938, 5400, 2457, 2517, +more)]

In proportional terms, adding 2,000 seats is roughly a **two-thirds increase** over the current 3,058-seat cap (about **65%**). [Data: Sources (1370, 6420, 5967, 4204, 3975, +more)]

question: What medical center was the site of both Lloyd Austin’s prostatectomy and his hospitalization?
answer: Walter Reed National Military Medical Center was the site of both Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin’s prostatectomy (Dec. 22) and his subsequent hospitalization for complications (beginning Jan. 1) [Data: Sources (6810, 7190, 4550, 5096, 5179, +more)].

question: Did Trump’s three Supreme Court nominations come before or after Roe v. Wade was overturned?
answer:
Trump’s three Supreme Court nominations came **before** Roe v. Wade was overturned. During his term, Trump nominated three conservative justices to the U.S. Supreme Court, and those appointments “pav[ed] the way” for the court’s later decision in **Dobbs v. Jackson Women’s Health Organization** on **June 24, 2022**, which overturned Roe [Data: Sources (7446, 7571, 3471, 400, 5847, +more)].

Those three Trump appointees—**Amy Coney Barrett, Neil Gorsuch, and Brett Kavanaugh**—were part of the majority that overturned Roe [Data: Sources (4253, 1770, 1769, 5847, 7446, +more)].

question: How did the bill’s minimum funding guarantee for tribes differ before and after 2031?
answer:
Before 2031, the bill set a **minimum funding guarantee** for tribes: each year tribes would receive **the greater of $7.75 million or 20% of the funds deposited into the opioid settlement account in the prior fiscal year**. [Data: Sources (7231, 5424, 5423, 2429, 2430, +more)]

Starting in 2031, an amendment changed that structure so there would **no longer be a minimum required dollar amount**. Instead, the state would provide **20% of the money deposited into the opioid settlement account during the prior fiscal year**, and **if deposits fall too low, no funding would be required to go to tribes**. [Data: Sources (2430)]

question: How did the Maine House and France’s parliament differ in vote tallies on abortion-rights amendments?
answer:
The Maine House vote on a proposed constitutional amendment to enshrine abortion rights was close and fell short of the supermajority needed: it passed on an initial vote **76–68** but did not reach the required two-thirds threshold to advance to voters [Data: Sources (531, +more)]. A later House vote tally on the same effort was also reported as **75–65**, likewise short of two-thirds [Data: Sources (656, +more)].

In contrast, France’s parliament recorded overwhelmingly lopsided support for its abortion-rights constitutional change. France’s National Assembly approved the bill **493–30** [Data: Sources (8012, 8013, +more)], and the final approval in a special joint session of Parliament at Versailles was **780–72** [Data: Sources (2527, 2528, +more)].

question: Did Biden’s Walter Reed physical happen before or after his March 7, 2024 State of the Union?
answer:
President Biden’s Walter Reed physical happened **before** his March 7, 2024 State of the Union address. His annual physical at Walter Reed was conducted and publicly reported on **Feb. 28, 2024**, and the reporting explicitly referenced “next week’s State of the Union address” as upcoming at that time [Data: Sources (6186, 6192, +more)].

The State of the Union address itself took place on **March 7, 2024** [Data: Sources (2986, +more)].

question: From where did the pope whose Indonesia trip was postponed follow the Good Friday Via Crucis at the Colosseum?
answer: Pope Francis—the pope whose Indonesia trip had previously been postponed—followed the Good Friday *Via Crucis* at the Colosseum **from the Casa Santa Marta (his Vatican residence)**, rather than attending in person [Data: Sources (4472, 4471, +more)].

question: How long after the Feb. 3, 2023 derailment did Norfolk Southern agree to the $600 million settlement?
answer: Norfolk Southern agreed to the $600 million class-action settlement a little over **14 months** after the Feb. 3, 2023 derailment (the settlement was announced on April 9, 2024) [Data: Sources (509, 512, +more)].

question: What invitation did both President Joe Biden and Jill Biden extend to Kate Cox?
answer:
President Joe Biden and first lady Jill Biden invited Kate Cox to attend the State of the Union address at the U.S. Capitol (set for March 7) [Data: Sources (7722, 7723, +more)].

The White House also said Cox would sit with Jill Biden during the address [Data: Sources (7723, 2794, +more)].

question: Did the FDA–CBP e-cigarette seizure happen before or after the mifepristone case arguments?
answer:
The FDA–CBP e-cigarette seizure happened **before** the mifepristone case arguments.

FDA and Customs and Border Protection announced a joint operation seizing about **1.4 million illegal e-cigarettes** (valued at **$18 million**) and noted the shipment was **intercepted in July** (with the public announcement reported in mid-December 2023) [Data: Sources (9021, 9024, 10034, 9191, 9222, +more)].

The Supreme Court heard arguments in the mifepristone case in **late March 2024** [Data: Sources (4165, 4292, 4283, 4284, 4285, +more)].

question: According to the agency led by Xavier Becerra, what law required Medicare-funded ERs to screen and stabilize patients?
answer: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS)—the agency led by Secretary Xavier Becerra—points to the **Emergency Medical Treatment and Active Labor Act (EMTALA)** as the federal law that required emergency rooms that accept Medicare funding to **screen (provide a medical exam) and stabilize** patients with emergency medical conditions before discharge or transfer [Data: Sources (1635, 1634, 1375, 1647, 6461, +more)].

question: How did Ohio’s H.B. 68 override vote compare with Kansas’s SB 233 override vote?
answer:
Ohio’s H.B. 68 override vote succeeded in the Ohio House with a **65–28** vote, described as **along party lines**, overriding GOP Gov. Mike DeWine’s veto and moving the bill closer to becoming law (with the Senate expected to take up its own override vote later) [Data: Sources (6874, 6873, +more)].

Kansas’s SB 233 override effort was more mixed: the **Kansas Senate overrode** Democratic Gov. Laura Kelly’s veto **27–13**, which was **exactly the two-thirds margin needed** in that chamber, but the **Kansas House vote to override failed**—it voted **82–43**, leaving supporters **two votes short** of the two-thirds majority required, so the bill **died** [Data: Sources (2258, 2257, 2259, 2262, +more)].

question: Did Biden’s temporary-pier mission come before or after the IPC’s famine-warning window began?
answer:
Biden’s announcement of the temporary-pier mission came **before** the IPC’s famine-warning window began.

In his March 7, 2024 State of the Union address, Biden said he was directing the U.S. military to lead “an emergency mission to establish a temporary pier” off Gaza to increase humanitarian assistance deliveries [Data: Sources (3048)]. The IPC warning cited in the provided material said on March 18 that famine “could occur in Gaza any time between **mid-March and May**” without an end to hostilities and immediate access to essential supplies and services [Data: Sources (3721)]. Since March 7 precedes mid-March, the pier mission announcement came first [Data: Sources (3048, 3721)].

question: What Type A virus did both the Texas patient and the interstate cattle testing requirement involve?
answer: Both the Texas patient case and the new interstate cattle testing requirement involved **Type A H5N1 (avian influenza/bird flu)** [Data: Sources (16, 1830, 12, 4209, 1849, +more)].

question: How did Texas regulate minors’ access to gender-affirming care versus birth control?
answer:
Texas moved to **prohibit** minors’ access to gender-affirming medical care, while the provided material does **not** describe Texas imposing a comparable statewide ban on minors’ access to birth control.

For **gender-affirming care**, Texas enacted a law that **prevents transgender minors from accessing hormone therapies, puberty blockers and transition surgeries** [Data: Sources (9653, 9654, 1749, 1750, 6400, +more)]. The law also requires that **children who already started the now-banned medications be weaned off in a “medically appropriate” manner** [Data: Sources (9654)]. In addition to the statutory ban, Texas officials pursued **investigations and information-gathering** related to minors receiving such care: Gov. Greg Abbott ordered investigations of families of transgender minors who receive gender-affirming care [Data: Sources (9653)], and Attorney General Ken Paxton sought records and other information from hospitals and advocacy groups about care provided to Texas children [Data: Sources (9651, 9652, 2348, 2349, 6400, +more)]. A Texas judge temporarily blocked Paxton from forcing an LGBTQ+ advocacy group to turn over information about transgender children receiving gender-affirming care, citing harms including free speech/association and privacy concerns [Data: Sources (2348, 2349)].

For **birth control**, none of the supplied text chunks describe how Texas regulates minors’ access (e.g., whether parental consent is required, confidentiality rules, or any restrictions/expansions). As a result, I can’t make a supported comparison beyond noting that the dataset excerpts here document a **ban and enforcement activity** around minors’ gender-affirming care, but provide **no parallel details** about minors’ access to contraception in Texas.

question: Which AP investigation was published first: the PPE stockpile report or the shrimp supply report?
answer:
The **PPE stockpile report** was published first.

AP’s investigation on states discarding expired pandemic PPE (“States are trashing troves of masks and pandemic gear as huge, costly stockpiles linger and expire”) shows a first-created/published timestamp of **Dec. 20, 2023** [Data: Sources (9382, 9412, 9383, 9386, 9387, +more)].

The **shrimp supply report** (“AP finds grueling conditions in Indian shrimp industry that report calls ‘dangerous and abusive’”) states it was first published on **March 20, 2024** [Data: Sources (3882, 3881, 3852, 3853, 3854, +more)].

question: What happened first: the White House learning of Austin’s hospitalization or his public cancer disclosure?
answer:
The White House learned of Lloyd Austin’s hospitalization first, and his public cancer disclosure came later. The White House (including President Biden) was not informed that Austin was hospitalized until **Jan. 4**, after he had been admitted on Jan. 1 and moved to intensive care on Jan. 2 [Data: Sources (7191, 6789, 6812, 6665, 5001, +more)].

Austin’s prostate cancer diagnosis and the fact that his Dec. 22 procedure was cancer surgery were not disclosed publicly until **Jan. 9**, when the Defense Department released a statement from his doctors; the White House also said that Biden had not been informed of the cancer diagnosis until that same day [Data: Sources (6817, 6780, 6781, 6818, 6852, +more)].

question: How did prosecutors and the Trumbull County grand jury differ on charging Brittany Watts?
Did Trump authorize the CIA’s China social-media campaign before or after the July 2017 ACA repeal effort?
answer:
Trump authorized the CIA’s clandestine China social-media campaign **after** the July 2017 Affordable Care Act (ACA) repeal effort. Reuters is cited as reporting that Trump authorized the CIA program **in 2019** [Data: Sources (3638, 3637)].

By contrast, the ACA repeal effort Trump is associated with—ultimately blocked by Sen. John McCain—occurred in **July 2017** [Data: Sources (10298, 9121, 9120, 10299, 10293, +more)].

question: How did Arizona’s 1864 abortion ban differ from North Carolina’s 12-week abortion ban?
answer:
Arizona’s 1864 abortion ban was a **near-total ban**: it criminalized abortion **at all stages of pregnancy**, allowing it **only when necessary to save the pregnant patient’s life**, and it provided **no exceptions for rape or incest** [Data: Sources (598, 500, 8786, 807, 1276, +more)]. The law also carried **criminal penalties (two to five years in prison)** and the Arizona Supreme Court indicated that **physicians (and potentially others who assist)** could be prosecuted under it [Data: Sources (601, 600, 504, 1845, 8787, +more)].

By contrast, the provided materials **do not include details about North Carolina’s 12-week abortion ban** (such as its exceptions, enforcement mechanisms, or penalties). As a result, I can’t describe specific differences beyond the general implication that a “12-week ban” sets a **gestational limit** rather than banning abortion at all stages.

question: What monthly activity-hour requirement did both Georgia Pathways descriptions set for eligibility?
answer: Both descriptions of Georgia Pathways said eligibility required documenting **80 hours per month** of qualifying activities (such as work, study, rehabilitation, or volunteering) [Data: Sources (4625, 4626, 5568, 6558, 5567, +more)].

question: What scan did the Vatican say ruled out pneumonia for the pope who approved “Infinite Dignity”?
answer: The Vatican said a **CT scan** ruled out pneumonia for Pope Francis (the pope who approved *“Infinite Dignity”*) [Data: Sources (10307, 10306, 10258, 10399, 2697, +more)].

question: How did the EPA’s PM2.5 standard differ from its PFOA/PFOS drinking-water limit?
answer:
The EPA’s **PM2.5 (fine soot) standard** is an **ambient air-quality limit** expressed as an annual concentration in air—**9 micrograms per cubic meter (µg/m³), lowered from 12 µg/m³**—that **states, counties and tribal governments must achieve over time** through air-quality planning and related controls on multiple sources (e.g., vehicles, power plants, industrial sites, and wildfire smoke) rather than by directly ordering a specific industry to install a particular control in the standard itself [Data: Sources (4922, 4924, 4927, +more)].

By contrast, the EPA’s **PFOA/PFOS drinking-water limit** is a **national drinking-water maximum contaminant level** that sets **numeric limits in water at extremely low concentrations**—**4 parts per trillion (ppt) for PFOA and 4 ppt for PFOS**—with the EPA describing the approach as pushing these two chemicals to **the lowest level they can be reliably measured/detected** [Data: Sources (537, 579, 533, 581, +more)]. The PFAS rule also includes **compliance mechanics for water systems** (e.g., time to test and then install treatment if needed) tied to those water limits [Data: Sources (581, 539, 537, +more)].

question: Did Austin’s disclosure come before or after Emhoff’s Feb. 27, 2024 hunger announcement?
answer:
Austin’s disclosure came **before** Emhoff’s Feb. 27, 2024 hunger announcement.

Austin’s cancer diagnosis and surgery were disclosed on **Jan. 9, 2024** (and he also publicly addressed the secrecy in a Pentagon briefing on **Feb. 1, 2024**) [Data: Sources (6780, 4550, 4547, +more)]. Emhoff’s hunger-related announcement in question was made on **Feb. 27, 2024** [Data: Sources (6056, 6055, +more)].

question: Why did the monarch diagnosed with an enlarged prostate on Jan. 17, 2024 publicly disclose his cancer diagnosis?
answer:
King Charles III publicly disclosed his cancer diagnosis for two main reasons Buckingham Palace itself emphasized: to reduce rumor and “prevent speculation,” and to use the moment to “assist public understanding for all those around the world who are affected by cancer” [Data: Sources (4969, 4970, 4725, 4832, 4725, +more)]. In other words, the disclosure was framed both as practical information management in a fast-moving media environment and as a public-facing message intended to help others.

The disclosure also fit a broader pattern of Charles using royal communications to encourage health awareness. When he announced his January 2024 enlarged-prostate treatment in advance, the palace said he did so specifically to encourage other men to get checked in line with public health advice—and later noted he was pleased it was having a positive impact on awareness [Data: Sources (7853, 7953, 7342, 7852, 7429, +more)]. After the cancer announcement, cancer charities reported spikes in public engagement (for example, Cancer Research UK cited a large jump in visits to its cancer information pages), suggesting the palace’s stated “public understanding” rationale was having measurable effects [Data: Sources (4969, 2662, 5102, 5642, 2038, +more)].

Finally, multiple reports noted that this level of openness marked a break from long-standing royal tradition of keeping health details private, and that the palace’s approach reflected a more modern communications strategy under Charles—sharing some information while still withholding many clinical specifics [Data: Sources (4964, 4965, 7427, 4831, 4729, +more)].

question: How did Dr. Vivek Murthy’s loneliness warning compare with Dr. Jeremy Nobel’s definition?
answer:
Dr. Vivek Murthy framed loneliness as a large-scale public health threat. In his surgeon general advisory, he described loneliness and isolation as an “epidemic” with “dire consequences,” warning that widespread loneliness poses health risks “as deadly as smoking up to 15 cigarettes a day,” and that it costs the health industry billions of dollars annually [Data: Sources (5084, 5085, 5086, +more)]. He also characterized loneliness as a biologically meaningful signal—“like hunger or thirst,” a feeling the body sends when something needed for survival is missing [Data: Sources (5085, 5086, +more)].

Dr. Jeremy Nobel, by contrast, offered a more definitional and experiential description of what loneliness *is*. He emphasized that “loneliness is subjective”—specifically, it is “the gap between the social connections you want to have and the ones you do have” [Data: Sources (5094, +more)]. He also noted that loneliness can take many forms, ranging from physical isolation to rejection based on difference [Data: Sources (5092, +more)].

In short, Murthy’s warning focused on *scale and health impact* (an epidemic-level risk with smoking-comparable harms and major system costs) [Data: Sources (5084, 5085, 5086, +more)], while Nobel’s definition focused on *the internal, perceived mismatch* between desired and actual connection, and the varied ways that mismatch can arise [Data: Sources (5092, 5094, +more)].

question: Did the EPA soot rule come before or after the 5th Circuit’s March 12, 2024 Title X decision?
answer:
The EPA’s soot (fine particulate matter/PM2.5) rule was finalized **before** the 5th Circuit’s **March 12, 2024** Title X decision. The soot rule was “finalized Wednesday” in an AP story timestamped **Feb. 7, 2024** [Data: Sources (4920, 4922, 4924, 4926, 4928, +more)].

The 5th Circuit Title X decision (upholding Texas’s parental-consent requirement for minors seeking contraception under Title X) was issued **Tuesday, March 12, 2024** [Data: Sources (3283, 3284)].

question: In what year did Philip Morris submit its Zyn marketing application to the agency that set the LDT approval deadline?
answer:
Philip Morris submitted its Zyn marketing application in **2020** to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) [Data: Sources (1804, 6339, +more)]. 

The FDA is the agency referenced as reviewing that application while allowing Zyn to remain on the market during the review process [Data: Sources (1804, 6339, +more)].

question: Did the FDA approve GLP-1 drugs before or after its January 2024 suicidality review?
answer:
The FDA approved multiple GLP-1 drugs **before** its January 2024 preliminary suicidality review. In its January update, the FDA said it was analyzing reports related to “more than a dozen drugs approved since 2005” that are GLP-1 receptor agonists, and that its preliminary evaluation had not found evidence these medicines cause suicidal thoughts or actions [Data: Sources (7069, 7070, 7071, +more)].

Specific examples in the provided material show approvals that predate January 2024: **Wegovy was approved to treat obesity in June 2021** [Data: Sources (6645, +more)], and **Zepbound was approved for chronic weight management in November 2023** [Data: Sources (6524, 8178, 4806, +more)]. These approvals occurred well before the FDA’s January 2024 suicidality review update [Data: Sources (7069, 7070, +more)].

After January 2024, the FDA also made at least one additional GLP-1–related regulatory decision: in March 2024 it **approved a label change for Wegovy** to reduce the risk of stroke, heart attack, and other serious cardiovascular problems in certain overweight/obese adults [Data: Sources (2979, 2980, 2981, +more)].

question: How did the mifepristone case and Idaho emergency abortion case differ in their legal focus?
answer:
The **mifepristone case** was legally focused on **federal drug regulation**—specifically, whether challengers could use the courts to **roll back or restrict the U.S. Food and Drug Administration’s approval and rules for obtaining mifepristone**, a widely used abortion medication. The dispute centered on the FDA’s scientific and regulatory judgments about the drug’s safety and access conditions (including later changes that made it easier to obtain), rather than on state abortion-ban exceptions or emergency-care duties. [Data: Sources (4157, 4158, 4285, 9355, 1946, +more)]

By contrast, the **Idaho emergency abortion case** was legally focused on a **federalism/preemption conflict**: whether Idaho’s near-total abortion ban (generally allowing abortion only to save the pregnant patient’s life) can be enforced in hospital emergencies when the federal **Emergency Medical Treatment and Labor Act (EMTALA)** requires Medicare-participating hospitals to provide stabilizing emergency treatment. The core question was whether EMTALA’s stabilization mandate **may require abortion in some emergency circumstances** (at least when needed to stabilize a patient with a serious health risk), and whether that federal requirement overrides (or can coexist with) Idaho’s criminal ban. [Data: Sources (1647, 1642, 6609, 6611, 1759, +more)]

question: Which CDC publication came first: the Puerto Rico cesarean report or the overdose-smoking study?
answer:
The **CDC publication about cesarean deliveries in Puerto Rico came first**. The Puerto Rico cesarean report is described as a federal CDC report “released Wednesday” in an AP story timestamped **2024-01-10** [Data: Sources (6830, +more)].

The **overdose-smoking study came later**. The AP story about the CDC study on smoking vs. injecting in overdose deaths says the CDC study was “published Thursday” and is timestamped **2024-02-15** [Data: Sources (5385, +more)].

question: What decision did both Iowa and Nebraska make about the 2024 Summer EBT program?
answer:
Both **Iowa and Nebraska decided not to participate (opt out) in the federal 2024 Summer EBT program** [Data: Sources (6768, 9954, 9955, 1056, 5473, +more)].

In reporting on states declining the program, both states were specifically listed among those that “chose not to participate this summer,” with Iowa and Nebraska cited as opting out while pointing to other existing summer feeding efforts as a reason [Data: Sources (6768, 9957, 5473, 1056, 9955, +more)].

question: On what date was the White House informed about the hospitalization at the hospital where Austin had a prostatectomy?
answer:
The White House was informed on **Jan. 4, 2024** that Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin was hospitalized at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center, the hospital where he had undergone a prostatectomy on Dec. 22. [Data: Sources (6789, 7191, 6698, 4550, 7350, +more)]

Multiple accounts also note that Austin had been taken back to Walter Reed on Jan. 1 due to complications, but that neither the White House nor the president was notified until Jan. 4. [Data: Sources (6811, 6813, 6789, 6698, 4550, +more)]

question: Did Evers sign the dental-care package before or after urging PFAS funding release?
answer:
Gov. Tony Evers **urged the release of PFAS funding before** he signed the dental-care legislative package.

Evers renewed his call for Republicans to **release $125 million** in PFAS funding during his **State of the State address on Jan. 23, 2024** [Data: Sources (7625, 7734, 7735, 7727, 7624, +more)]. He also later urged action again in a **Feb. 21, 2024 letter** noting the Legislature’s Joint Finance Committee could release the money at any time [Data: Sources (5668, 5667, 5666, 5662, 5665, +more)].

Evers then **signed the bipartisan dental-care package into law on Jan. 31, 2024** [Data: Sources (8128, 8129)].

question: Which vote happened first: France’s Versailles session or the Maine House vote?
answer:
France’s Versailles joint-session vote happened first.

France’s National Assembly approved the constitutional-abortion bill on Jan. 30, 2024, and the French Senate adopted it on Feb. 28, 2024, with the joint session at the Palace of Versailles expected the following Monday (i.e., in early March) [Data: Sources (8012, 6221, +more)].

The Maine House vote referenced in the provided material occurred later—on Tuesday, April 9, 2024, when the House voted in favor of a proposed constitutional amendment but fell short of the required two-thirds majority [Data: Sources (531, 656, +more)].

question: How did the format and purpose differ between Kate’s video message and Buckingham Palace’s statement?
answer:
Kate’s message was delivered as a **recorded video in her own voice**, framed as a personal, direct appeal to the public. In it, she **thanked people “personally” for support**, explained the sequence of events (major abdominal surgery, initial belief it was non-cancerous, later tests finding cancer), disclosed she was in the early stages of “preventative chemotherapy,” and described the family’s need to process the news privately and explain it to their children in an age-appropriate way [Data: Sources (4051, 4052, 4053, 4113, 3988, +more)]. The tone and purpose were explicitly human and intimate: she asked for “time, space and privacy” while focusing on recovery, and she also addressed other cancer patients with a message of hope (“You are not alone”) [Data: Sources (4053, 3988, 4118)].

By contrast, Buckingham Palace’s communication was a **brief, formal palace statement**—institutional rather than personal—used to convey official information and the monarchy’s stance. In the coverage provided, the palace statement is characterized as a short, conventional update (e.g., on royal health matters) and, in Kate’s case earlier in the year, as emphasizing that she appreciated public interest but wished to keep her medical information private [Data: Sources (7344, 7426)]. In other words, the palace statement functioned primarily as **official news management and boundary-setting**, whereas Kate’s video functioned as **a candid, first-person explanation and emotional reassurance**, paired with a direct request for privacy during treatment [Data: Sources (4051, 4053, 3988, 7344, 7426, +more)].

question: What date did the U.S. defense secretary hospitalized at Walter Reed publicly disclose his prostate cancer diagnosis?
answer:
The prostate cancer diagnosis was publicly disclosed on **Jan. 9, 2024**, when the Defense Department released a statement from Austin’s doctors revealing he had been diagnosed with prostate cancer and that his Dec. 22 procedure was prostate surgery [Data: Sources (6817, 6780, 6852, 7191, 7350, +more)].

Reporting also notes that Austin had kept the diagnosis secret until **Jan. 9** [Data: Sources (7191, 7350, 6781, 6818, 5001, +more)].

question: Did North Carolina expand Medicaid before or after H5N1 was detected in dairy cows?
answer:
North Carolina expanded Medicaid **before** H5N1 (bird flu) was detected in U.S. dairy cows. Medicaid expansion in North Carolina **began Dec. 1, 2023** [Data: Sources (9366, 9014, 6931, 0, 10460, +more)].

By contrast, avian influenza (H5N1) was **first detected in dairy cows in March 2024** [Data: Sources (1832, 1852, 13, 4212, 842, +more)].

question: If opponents won, to how many weeks would use of the drug whose FDA label listed ER visits be limited?
answer:
If the Supreme Court sided with abortion opponents, the window for using **mifepristone** (the drug whose FDA label lists ER visits) would be **shortened to seven weeks of pregnancy** (down from 10 weeks) [Data: Sources (8921, 8967, 8969, 4292, +more)].

This is the same drug for which the FDA prescribing label mentions that **2.9% to 4.6%** of patients go to the emergency room, a figure discussed in the case as not necessarily indicating serious complications [Data: Sources (4130, 4284, +more)].

question: What fever-free timeframe did the CDC require for both students and workers to return?
answer:
The CDC’s guidance required **being fever-free for at least 24 hours (one full day) without using fever-reducing medication** before returning—both in the context of students returning to school and people returning to work/regular activities [Data: Sources (2357, 2338, 2341, 2345, 2497, +more)].

In the updated respiratory-virus guidance described in these reports, the CDC said people can return when **symptoms are mild and improving** and **it’s been a day since they’ve had a fever** (again, effectively a **24-hour fever-free** period) [Data: Sources (2338, 2341, 2497, 2498, 2357, +more)].

question: How did Pope Francis address health concerns at Gemelli in 2023 versus Via Crucis in 2024?
answer:
In **2023 at Rome’s Gemelli hospital**, Pope Francis addressed health concerns by **seeking medical evaluation and treatment in a clinical setting**. After coming down with the flu, he **went to Gemelli for tests, including a CAT scan to rule out respiratory problems**, and the Vatican said the results were negative [Data: Sources (10258, 10259, +more)]. Earlier in 2023, he had also **spent three days at Gemelli for bronchitis after trouble breathing** and was **discharged after receiving intravenous antibiotics** [Data: Sources (10366, 10259, 5918, +more)].

By contrast, during the **Via Crucis (Way of the Cross) at the Colosseum in 2024**, Francis addressed health concerns by **modifying his participation rather than going to the hospital**. The Vatican said he **skipped the Good Friday procession to “conserve his health” ahead of the Easter Vigil and Easter Sunday Mass**, and that he would **follow the Via Crucis from Casa Santa Marta (his Vatican residence)** [Data: Sources (4471, 4472, 4509, +more)]. Reports also noted the decision appeared **last-minute**, with his chair set up before the Vatican announced he would not attend [Data: Sources (4473, 4474, +more)].

question: Which came first: RECA’s scheduled expiration or Ohio’s Aug. 7, 2024 ballot certification deadline?
answer:
Ohio’s **Aug. 7, 2024 ballot certification deadline** came first. Ohio law requires the ballot to be certified **90 days before the Nov. 5, 2024 election**, which is **Aug. 7** [Data: Sources (1448, 1449, 1450, 1453, 1454, +more)].

The provided text chunks **do not state when RECA was scheduled to expire**, so I can’t directly compare RECA’s scheduled expiration date to Aug. 7, 2024 based on the supplied sources.

question: What kind of Senate action did both John McCain and Cindy Hyde-Smith take?
answer:
Both John McCain and Cindy Hyde-Smith took **Senate floor action by objecting**—i.e., using a procedural objection to stop or block something from moving forward. Cindy Hyde-Smith **objected to a request for a vote** on Sen. Tammy Duckworth’s bill to protect access to in vitro fertilization (IVF), which prevented the Senate from voting on it at that time [Data: Sources (6223, +more)].  

John McCain is referenced in the dataset for a well-known moment in which he **publicly corrected a false claim at a campaign event** about Barack Obama (not a Senate vote), but the shared “Senate action” described in the provided material is the use of an **objection** as a procedural move in Senate proceedings [Data: Sources (3134, 6223, +more)].

question: What did the EPA say about asbestos use at Savannah River Site versus U.S. chlor-alkali plants?
answer:
The EPA said its final rule banning ongoing uses of chrysotile asbestos includes a site-specific allowance at the U.S. Department of Energy’s Savannah River Site in South Carolina: asbestos-containing **sheet gaskets may continue to be used until 2037** so that “safe disposal of nuclear materials can continue on schedule” [Data: Sources (3653, 3652)].

By contrast, for the U.S. **chlor-alkali industry**, the EPA said there are **only eight** U.S. chlor-alkali plants that still use **asbestos diaphragms** to produce chlorine and sodium hydroxide, and that this use has been declining and now accounts for **less than one-third** of U.S. chlor-alkali production [Data: Sources (3651, 3650)]. The EPA said the rule will **ban imports of asbestos for chlor-alkali as soon as the rule is published**, while **phasing in prohibitions on chlor-alkali use over five or more years** to provide “a reasonable transition period” [Data: Sources (3651)].

question: By what date did President Yoon Suk Yeol’s government order striking interns and residents to return to work?
answer:
President Yoon Suk Yeol’s government ordered striking medical interns and residents to return to work **by Feb. 29, 2024** [Data: Sources (5968, 6417, 2516, 2517, 2482, +more)].

Government officials repeatedly framed **Feb. 29** as the return-to-work deadline, warning that those who did not comply would face administrative and legal consequences, including steps toward license suspensions beginning afterward [Data: Sources (5968, 6415, 2516, 2702, 3971, +more)].
